prompt: Create a HarborOps Operations & Compliance platform that unifies facility asset control, foodservice production governance, and internal collaboration for organizations that run cafeterias and shared equipment across multiple sites. Users access an English React web interface tailored by role: Staff (asset custodians and kitchen leads) can sign in with a local username and password, submit real-name profiles, and work in guided workflows for maintaining assets and food content; Couriers can be enabled only for internal delivery handoffs and are restricted to viewing assigned pickup/drop tasks and sign-in confirmations; Administrators manage tenants, permissions, and audits. The onboarding UI captures legal name, employee or student ID, and a photo ID file upload for manual review; accounts remain in “Pending Review” until an Admin approves, after which the system shows a time-stamped status history and disables sensitive actions for any “Suspended” account. Asset ledger screens support classification and coding rules with live validation and duplicate warnings, letting Staff bulk import/export spreadsheets, correct rejected rows inline, and browse an “as-of” timeline to see prior versions of an asset record. Kitchen users manage dish master data with portion specs and add-ons, allergen flags and nutrition panels, and per-serving cost rollups from recipe ingredients; recipes include step-by-step instructions, ingredient quantities in US units, versioning, and effective date ranges, while menus support grouping, sorting, publish/unpublish controls, time-of-day availability windows (for example 6:00 AM–10:30 AM breakfast), and bulk release to specific sites. A meeting workspace supports agenda collection, offline material distribution, attendance sign-in, minutes and resolutions, and converting resolutions into assigned tasks with due dates and progress status, keeping an auditable chain from meeting to execution. The backend uses Django to enforce role-based permission isolation, validation rules, and workflow state machines, with MySQL persisting all operational data locally so the system remains fully functional without an internet connection. Authentication uses salted password hashing and configurable lockout (for example, 5 failed attempts triggers a 15-minute lock), and sensitive fields such as government ID numbers are stored encrypted at rest with masked display in the UI. Bulk imports run server-side format checks, required-field validation, and deterministic deduplication using a local fingerprint of key attributes; accepted rows create new version snapshots rather than overwriting history. Menu and recipe calculations are performed server-side with rounding rules (currency to cents) and effective-date enforcement so only one active version applies at a time per site. The system exposes internal REST-style APIs that the React frontend consumes over a decoupled architecture, using consistent pagination, versioned endpoints, and signed requests to prevent tampering; optional local webhooks and a lightweight message queue can notify other on-prem systems, with idempotency keys and retry policies (for example, 3 retries over 10 minutes) to avoid duplicate processing. Analytics and monitoring are computed from local logs and transactional tables, including funnel metrics (draft-to-published menus), asset utilization and exception counts, and job/API health; anomalies generate alerts that must be acknowledged, assigned, and closed with a resolution note to complete the alert closed loop.
